name: GoA Design System Governance
summary: How teams work with the design system - process, contribution criteria, and collaboration

corePhilosophy.designSystemFirst: Start with existing components and patterns - covers ~80% of service needs
corePhilosophy.userDriven: Changes driven by usability testing and real user needs, not preferences
corePhilosophy.collaborative: Work with design system team for decisions and knowledge sharing

communicationChannels: Design System Support channel on Slack

workflowSteps.name: Use the Design System as Default
workflowSteps.description: Start with existing components - should cover ~80% of needs

workflowSteps.name: Identify User Need Not Handled
workflowSteps.description: Through usability testing, discover when existing solutions don't meet user needs

workflowSteps.name: Talk to Design System Team
workflowSteps.description: Collaborate to understand options and make informed decisions

workflowSteps.name: Apply Problem-Solving Hierarchy
workflowSteps.description: 1) Change Content → 2) Change Layout → 3) Design New Components

workflowSteps.name: Test New Solution with Users
workflowSteps.description: Validate solutions through user feedback and testing

workflowSteps.name: Share Learnings with Design System
workflowSteps.description: Solutions either added to system or tracked for future consideration

problemSolvingHierarchy.principle: Start simple with content before applying UI solutions

contributionCriteria.useful: Evidence that component would be useful for many teams or services
contributionCriteria.unique: Does not replicate something already in the design system
contributionCriteria.better: Creates better experience, informed by research
contributionCriteria.universal: Meets or exceeds WCAG 2.1 accessibility standards

teamRoles.designSystemTeam: Guide component selection, review compliance, provide expertise, decide what enters shared system
teamRoles.productTeam: Define user needs, implement with GoA components, share learnings back
teamRoles.note: Product teams can do what they need; design system team decides what becomes shared

riskIndicators: Building custom components that already exist
Working in isolation without design system consultation
Creating solutions that don't meet accessibility standards
